content:
Is anybody working on platform device and devicetree abstractions for recent RFL rust-dev/-next branches? Like it is available in the (outdated) rust branch:

https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/linux/blob/rust/rust/kernel/platform.rs
https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/linux/blob/rust/rust/kernel/of.rs

I searched the open PRs on github and the mailing list for 'devicetree' but couldn't find anything relevant. Just the old PRs which where merged into rust branch. Or have I missed anything?

I was wondering about it too since this says DRM subsystem maintainer is interested in merging a DRM diver(I'm guessing asahi) and that would require platform dev abstractions.
I'm working off a franken-kernel based off the old rust branch because these abstractions are missing in mainline and rust-next

I tried to port these abstractions to rust-next for my work on regulators. Most of these abstractions work fine on rust-next/rustc 1.73.0 but the drivers.rs abstraction do not work with rustc 1.73.0 anymore. So in my branch I reverted back to 1.72.1.

The issue is a compilation error because of const_trait_impl

It would be great if someone had a fix for it.
I guess const trait stuff will not be fixed until keyword generics are implemented :/ but good to know platform dev stuff works, I should spend some time rebasing my code

Fabien Parent said:

I tried to port these abstractions to rust-next for my work on regulators. Most of these abstractions work fine on rust-next/rustc 1.73.0 but the drivers.rs abstraction do not work with rustc 1.73.0 anymore. So in my branch I reverted back to 1.72.1.

The issue is a compilation error because of const_trait_impl

It would be great if someone had a fix for it.

I have a version of DriverOps and Registration rebased on upstream kernel here. It is part of the nvme driver effort.
Its for linux-6.6 so it will build with rustc 1.71.1.
There is not really a lot of changes in the PR for 6.7, so it is probably fine for you to stay on 6.6 ?
I think the issue Fabien hit is related to https://github.com/rust-lang/rust/issues/110395
or this one: https://github.com/rust-lang/rust/issues/67792
Looks like Martin already reported this: https://rust-lang.zulipchat.com/#narrow/stream/146212-t-compiler.2Fconst-eval/topic/Calling.20const_trait.20method.20in.20impl's.20const.20fn/near/399162928
Do we use this in tree already? I think we need to avoid using nightly features related to const traits, their design is extremely volatile and the implementation is completely experimental
Workarounds with macros and standalone const functions are about the best we can do there

Fabien Parent said:

The issue is a compilation error because of const_trait_impl

It would be great if someone had a fix for it.

I'm trying to see a fix for this too, what is happening is that const_trait_impl requires effects but if you add it you'll still see a compiler error. I've made this Zulip thread in rust-lang to see if they pick it up. I've made some discoveries there but still I don't know how to fix it. :face_with_diagonal_mouth:

https://rust-lang.zulipchat.com/#narrow/stream/146212-t-compiler.2Fconst-eval/topic/Calling.20const_trait.20method.20in.20impl's.20const.20fn/near/399162928

Trevor Gross said:

Do we use this in tree already? I think we need to avoid using nightly features related to const traits, their design is extremely volatile and the implementation is completely experimental

In my local I've entirely omitted DeviceId handling for now, some things should be worked on like, for instance, depmod not looking into our id tables.
From reading the RFC issue, it seems like#![feature(const_trait_impl)] is buggy and not ready for actual production use. I agree with Trevor, lets rewrite with macro instead.
Or at it has known bugs that you need to know about in order to use it without breaking stuff

Here I have my branch with both device and driver abstractions, check them out and tell me what do you think about it.

https://github.com/YakoYakoYokuYoku/linux/commits/driver-rust

Andreas Hindborg said:

Or at it has known bugs that you need to know about in order to use it without breaking stuff

I think it is missing device::Data, device::RawDevice, driver::RawDeviceId and all the device id table creation macros. They are used by NVMe.

I have to rebase NVMe on the 6.7 PR or at least when linux 6.7 is released, so I need to figure out a solution if const_trait is no longer usable. So if someone is going to fix that problem, please let me know so we don't do double work.
Sorry it looks like I replied to the wrong post. This was supposed to be a reply to your last comment @Martin Rodriguez Reboredo

I can introduce support for device::Data, although I've omitted device::RawDevice on purpose because I've used some changes made by Gary, in those you use AsRef<Device> for printing instead. I'd prefer that the device ID bindings are sent after either my reported issue is solved or another workaround is found.

I fixed the const_trait_impl stuff the other day, though it's not pretty...

https://github.com/AsahiLinux/linux/commit/71e6c52a71d38bbbccccf8eeb2cfcdc58421df4a
Seems fair.
Just for the logs, I think this https://rust-for-linux.zulipchat.com/#narrow/stream/291566-Library/topic/i2c.20subsystem/near/405043487 is what I was looking for. Thanks!

Asahi Lina said:

I fixed the const_trait_impl stuff the other day, though it's not pretty...

https://github.com/AsahiLinux/linux/commit/71e6c52a71d38bbbccccf8eeb2cfcdc58421df4a

Thanks! I included it in the rebase of rnvme for 6.7. I hope they bring back the const traits soon so we don't have to carry workarounds like this for long.

I fixed the const_trait_impl stuff the other day, though it's not pretty...

https://github.com/AsahiLinux/linux/commit/71e6c52a71d38bbbccccf8eeb2cfcdc58421df4a

As that patch removes 

-    fn to_rawid(&self, offset: isize) -> Self::RawType;

it looks to me that rustdoc is failing as line ~120 isn't removed, too:

///  - [`RawDeviceId::to_rawid`] stores `offset` in the context/data field of the raw device id so
///     that buses can recover the pointer to the data.

So I think that patch needs an update to remove that line, too?

Just fyi as it looks like there are are several users of that patch, now :wink:
Docstrings need to be fixed up in a few places :+1: But we won't be carrying this forever.

Looking around for some existing abstraction to read a DeviceTree I found

https://github.com/AsahiLinux/linux/commit/9e496b356ee8e25f9bee9258491aa6ae3a4f1ddf

in the AsahiLinux tree.

I picked that on top of Fabo's v6.7-rc1 based rust-platform branch and at least it seems to build fine :wink:

Before I find out how to use that (some examples?) I wonder if this is the most recent one to use for my experiments? Or if I missed anything else? The old RFL rust branch has only the DeviceID/RawDeviceID stuff, but not the DeviceTree reading functionality.
Hello everyone. I was wondering what the status of devicetree abstractions is for Rust. With the merge of arm64 support, this can be quite helpful since devicetree are used a lot for arm drivers.

This just got posted to mailing list: https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/Zfsj0_tb-0-tNrJy@cassiopeiae/T/#u
Provdes lot of useful abstractions.

I've been maintaining my own branch for awhile for arm64 development: https://github.com/Fabo/linux/commits/fparent/rust-platform.

Contains everything I need that I cherry-picked from rust, mailing-list, and other developer branches.

Here an example of one for my WIP driver branch that uses it: https://github.com/Fabo/linux/commits/fparent/rust-ncv6336.

Maybe some of these can help you.

Fabien Parent said:

Here an example of one for my WIP driver branch that uses it: https://github.com/Fabo/linux/commits/fparent/rust-ncv6336.

I really appreciate what Fabien does there. I use it a lot as base and inspiration for my own work. So many thanks for that!

As far as I understood the device tree abstraction included there (rust/kernel/of.rs) contain the basics needed for defining "compatible" to make a driver being probed based on that. That's great! :grinning:

I extended that to be able to additionally read properties from the device tree node the driver might need to configure itself. The best I could find so far for that is rust: of: Add OF node abstraction what picks relatively cleanly on top of Fabien's work.

Hi everyone.

I wonder what is missing here? Any reason in particular why platform support wasn't merged upstream yet?

@Asahi Lina Hi Lina,

https://github.com/AsahiLinux/linux/commit/c7f800e9f3df59f9e387dad2d944eb09f513db2c

Commit reference: 3dfc5eb

This seems to point to an unrelated commit?

Daniel Almeida said:

I wonder what is missing here?

I would guess people are busy with other (basic abstraction) stuff? So most probably nobody is working on this?

@Dirk Behme This seems to have been picked in a few different downstream trees. It looks like it's working, so I am asking whether there's a major blocker preventing this form being merged.

For example, I see it depends on a feature, is this what is blocking it?

@Daniel Almeida It required the abstractions I'm working on in the device / driver series [1], I'm still discussing with Greg about.

[1] https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/20240618234025.15036-1-dakr@redhat.com/

Dirk Behme said:

Daniel Almeida said:

I wonder what is missing here?

I would guess people are busy with other (basic abstraction) stuff? So most probably nobody is working on this?

Yeah - I wanted to work on it at some point but I've been sidetracked working on other stuff
Has anyone started rebasing platform device abstraction onto 6.11-rc or staging/rust-dev? I started to look at it for asahi/6.11
I have the platform device abstraction on top of rust-next (6.11-rc4) over there https://github.com/Fabo/linux/commits/fparent/rust-ncv6336/

Hello everyone. I have recently been working on adding mikrobus addon boards support to upstream kernel. More information about how it will be done can be found in the following patch series.
However, to keep it short, the support is heavily based on device tree and will only have a platform driver stub in the begining. This stub driver will later be expanded to allow dynamic devicetree loading using sysfs entry and reading 1-wire eeprom in boards with it. But the basic board support just needs a stub driver, to allow acess to nexus nodes, and nothing more.
I have also been interested in Rust for Linux and thought that this might be a good driver to upstream platform device and driver abstractions. After going through the recent discussions in the mailing list, it seems like a generic driver registration or id table patch will not be accepted. So, I modified @Fabien Parent patch to remove dependence on the generic abstactions.
I would like it if people more familiar with Rust for Linux can help find any obvious flaws with my patches: https://github.com/Ayush1325/linux/commits/b4/mikrobus-dt/
